mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) in a linear regression against FEDFUNDS changes over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where the unemployment rate rises by over 0.3 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion that drives corporate revenue growth, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive change in retail sales and housing starts fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns with a rank IC greater than 0.02 over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average reflects contracting manufacturing output that triggers short-term equity sell-offs, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in over 60% of observations when industrial production drops below its 12-month trend during tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly narrowing of the spread between the federal funds rate and annualized CPI inflation reduces real discount rates, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The narrowing real rate spread fails to predict positive 20-day forward returns with a statistically significant coefficient (p < 0.05) in a multivariate regression over a 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly declines in total nonfarm payrolls signal weakening economic activity that reduces aggregate corporate profit forecasts, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show a positive correlation with payroll declines, or the rank IC remains below -0.01 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP growth rate exceeding 2.5 percent confirms robust economic expansion that boosts equity risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of quarters where real GDP growth surpasses 2.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent monthly declines in industrial production and increases in the unemployment rate during a recessionary regime signal severe supply-side contraction that triggers broad equity deleveraging, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines and UNRATE rises simultaneously
failure_condition: The joint deterioration in industrial production and unemployment fails to predict negative 20-day forward returns with a win rate below 45% during NBER-defined recessions.
